title: Harden Work Item branch, PR, and closure workflow
outOfScope: Changing PR provider authentication or automatic PR creation/merge behavior.
Changing archived evidence, archive retention, or unrelated governance checks.
Changing the repository default branch or requiring a provider-specific CLI workflow.

sources.reason: Repository rules define the Work Item, PR, merge, cleanup, and closure lifecycle.
Cockpit workflow documentation is a user-facing governance entry point.
Reference workflow describes base synchronization and lifecycle closure.
First Work Item guide is the practical workflow used by collaborators.
Finish command is the executable boundary before archiving a Work Item.
Closure command owns merged-PR verification, branch deletion, and base synchronization.

riskAssessment.reason: Narrow guard and documentation changes around an existing fail-closed closure command; no archive or PR provider behavior changes.

executionDecision.reason: User approved the design direction: enforce the canonical PR-merge-then-close lifecycle and prevent base-branch finish.

preReviewWarnings: Review that the branch guard discovers the repository base branch without assuming a provider-specific default.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: ai-finish refuses to run on the discovered repository base branch with an actionable message and does not archive the Work Item.
The canonical lifecycle is documented: latest remote base, dedicated Work Item branch, finish/archive, push, PR, merge, then ai-close-work-item for branch deletion and base synchronization.
The workflow explicitly forbids merging the feature branch into local main before the PR and forbids deleting the Work Item branch before lifecycle closure.
ai-close-work-item explains that closure must be run while the merged Work Item branch is still identifiable when invoked from the base branch or after premature deletion.
Regression tests assert the base-branch finish guard, the lifecycle closure diagnostic, and unchanged normal-branch closure behavior.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The previous archive-threshold Work Item exposed a repeatable lifecycle failure: the feature branch was auto-deleted during PR merge, closure was attempted from local main, local main temporarily became ahead of origin/main, and the expected order of PR merge, closure cleanup, and base synchronization was not enforced by executable guidance.
constraints: Preserve historical evidence and existing fail-closed closure checks.
Keep the workflow generic across Git hosting providers and remote/default-branch names.
Use one Work Item, one dedicated branch, and one PR; do not broaden this change into PR automation.
intent.rationale: Make the intended lifecycle unambiguous in the rules and documentation, then add a small executable guard at ai-finish so a Work Item cannot be finalized from the base branch. Improve closure diagnostics so an incorrect order points to the exact recovery action.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: ai-finish invoked on the discovered repository base branch
scenarioCoverage.reason: To be covered by the new finish-command regression test.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: ai-finish invoked on a dedicated Work Item branch
scenarioCoverage.reason: To be covered by existing finish-command tests and the final verification run.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: ai-close-work-item invoked after branch deletion or from base branch
scenarioCoverage.reason: To be covered by the lifecycle-closure regression test.
Existing lifecycle closure tests must remain green.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly confirmed continuation after the preflight review; this Work Item patches the repository operating rules and Cockpit workflow documentation.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
